subject: Sociology
title: The Middle Class Squeeze
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Middle Class Squeeze.

hook.text: Your parents bought a house at 30 with one salary. You're 35, dual income, and still renting. The middle is shrinking.

buildup.text: The middle class once defined American life: stable jobs, homeownership, and upward mobility for children.

discovery.text: Since the 1970s, wages stagnated while costs for housing, healthcare, and education skyrocketed.

twist.text: Productivity doubled since 1980, but workers' pay barely moved. The gains went to shareholders and executives.

climax.text: The middle class isn't vanishing equally. Some rose into comfort; more slid toward economic precarity.

punchline.text: The middle class didn't fail. The economy moved without it.

quiz.question: What has happened to the middle class since the 1970s?
options: Wages stagnated while living costs rose sharply
It was abolished by government policy
